news.headline: Is The Power Grid Now Nvidia's Biggest Growth Constraint?
news.summary: Consensus has Nvidia (NVDA) growing revenue by roughly 70% this year and by over 30% next year. The demand is real, and the capex behind it is committed. What is not well modeled is the infrastructure risk sitting between that demand and actual revenue recognition. The power grid is the primary constraint, and it is already affecting deployment schedules in 2026.

news.headline: Xanadu Quantum Revenue Increases Fourfold. Why the Stock Is Falling.

news.headline: IonQ and 5 More Stocks to Play Quantum Fever
news.summary: Quantum computing has graduated from a theoretical curiosity to a stock market phenomenon.  Quantum computers, offshoots of quantum mechanics, can explore multiple solutions at once, and promise the kind of processing power that could revolutionize medicine, materials science, and other fields.  The pure-play stocks—the shares of companies that concentrate their efforts wholly on quantum computing—trade mostly on sentiment, since none of the companies have yet to turn a profit.

news.headline: Samsung strike looms: Why analysts say to “watch MU” as memory fabs warm down
news.summary: Investing.com -- Samsung Electronics has entered emergency management mode as up to 50,000 employees prepare to strike for 18 days starting May 21, according to Mizuho TMT Sector Specialist Jordan Klein. The company has begun a "warm down" of its memory fabs at the Pyeongtaek facility to prevent equipment damage during a potential stoppage.

news.headline: Micron Rally Puts Chip ETFs in Focus
news.summary: Micron's big AI run has some investors looking at ETFs instead of betting on one stock.
